title: 10 best herbal essential oils for pertussis
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for pertussis are plant-based extracts used to alleviate the symptoms and complications of pertussis, also known as whooping cough. These oils help by reducing inflammation, fighting off bacterial infections, and soothing respiratory issues. Some of the best herbal essential oils for pertussis include Eucalyptus globulus, Echinacea purpurea, Echinacea angustifolia, Thymus vulgaris, Melaleuca alternifolia, Zingiber officinale, Echinacea pallida, Lavandula angustifolia, Pelargonium sidoides, and Rosmarinus officinalis. If left untreated, pertussis can lead to prolonged coughing fits, respiratory failure, and even death, severely impacting daily life and causing immense suffering for individuals and their loved ones.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower essential oils helps with pertussis because of its natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil contains compounds like triterpenoid saponins, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause pertussis. Additionally, the oil's soothing effects can help reduce coughing fits and inflammation in the respiratory tract, making it easier for the body to fight off the infection. By using kansas coneflower essential oils, individuals may experience relief from pertussis symptoms, promoting a faster recovery time.
remedy_properties: Immunomodulatory effects: Echinacea's ability to modulate the immune response can help boost the body's natural defenses against infections like pertussis, reducing the severity and duration of symptoms.
Antioxidant properties: Echinacea contains a range of antioxidants that may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by pertussis, which can aid in symptom relief and promote recovery.
Anti-inflammatory effects: Echinacea's anti-inflammatory compounds may help alleviate coughing, congestion, and other respiratory symptoms associated with pertussis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Kansas coneflower contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to have antimicrobial properties and may help alleviate symptoms of pertussis.

Seeds: The seeds of the Kansas coneflower are rich in fatty acids and terpenoids, which can be extracted to produce essential oils that may help reduce inflammation and congestion associated with pertussis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh echinacea angustifolia roots and leaves on a dry morning.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm, dark place for 7-10 days.
Combine 20g of dried echinacea angustifolia roots and 10g of leaves with 100ml of high-proof vodka in a glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2 weeks, shaking daily, before filtering and discarding solids.
Distill the infused vodka using a still or steam distillation apparatus to produce echinacea angustifolia essential oil with a yield of approximately 1%.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme essential oils helps with pertussis because of its natural antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. When inhaled, thyme oil's active compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, directly target the Bordetella pertussis bacteria that cause the respiratory infection. This can help reduce symptoms like coughing, congestion, and difficulty breathing by loosening mucus and reducing inflammation in the airways. Additionally, thyme's decongestant properties may also help alleviate wheezing and shortness of breath associated with pertussis.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Thyme essential oil's antibacterial properties can help combat the bacterial infection caused by Bordetella pertussis, the bacterium responsible for pertussis (whooping cough).

Expectorant properties: Thyme essential oil's expectorant properties can help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing the severity of symptoms such as coughing and wheezing.

Antispasmodic properties: Thyme essential oil's antispasmodic properties can help relax bronchial muscles and reduce spasms caused by pertussis, which can help alleviate coughing fits and improve breathing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots are rich in volatile compounds that have antibacterial and antispasmodic properties, making them effective against the bacterial cause of pertussis.
Leaves: Thyme leaves contain high amounts of cineole, a compound that helps to open airways and alleviate respiratory issues associated with pertussis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves at dawn when dew is still present on the plant surfaces carefully collecting 50g of clean and healthy leaves.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm, well-ventilated area away from direct sunlight for 7-10 days or until they reach a moisture level of 15%.
Steam distill the dried thymus vulgaris leaves using a clevenger apparatus with 1000ml of water and 50g of leaves at a temperature of 80°c for 2 hours.
Collect and filter the resulting essential oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into dark glass bottles avoiding direct sunlight and heat.
Store the thymus vulgaris essential oil in a cool, dry place away from children and pets protected from light and contamination for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree essential oils helps with pertussis because of its antimicrobial properties, which combat the bacteria that cause the disease. The oil's antiviral and anti-inflammatory effects also help to reduce symptoms such as coughing, fever, and congestion. Additionally, tea tree essential oil's natural expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe and expel the infection. Its ability to boost the immune system further enhances its effectiveness in treating pertussis.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Tea Tree Essential Oil has potent antimicrobial activity against bacteria, viruses, and fungi, which can help combat the Bordetella pertussis bacteria that causes whooping cough.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Tea Tree Essential Oil's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce inflammation and congestion in the lungs, making it easier to breathe and potentially reducing the severity of whooping cough symptoms.
Expectorant properties: Tea Tree Essential Oil has natural expectorant properties that can help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, relieving coughing fits and making it easier to expel phlegm.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the primary source of essential oil in tea trees, and their high concentration of cineole makes them effective against pertussis bacteria.
Barks: The barks contain a significant amount of sesquiterpenes, which have antimicrobial properties that can help combat pertussis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried leaves of melaleuca alternifolia.
Dry the harvested material in a well-ventilated area for 7-10 days.
Combine one part dried melaleuca alternifolia leaves with five parts carrier oil such as sweet almond oil or coconut oil.
Steep the mixture in a dark glass jar at room temperature for 14-21 days, shaking occasionally.
Strain and filter the mixture through cheesecloth or coffee filters to produce a herbal extract.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon essential oils helps with pertussis because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The oil's active compounds, such as cinnamaldehyde, have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause pertussis, including Bordetella pertussis. Additionally, Ceylon cinnamon essential oil has been found to reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, which can help alleviate symptoms such as coughing and difficulty breathing associated with pertussis.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Cinnamon's antibacterial properties can help combat pertussis-causing bacteria (Bordetella pertussis) by inhibiting their growth and replication.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Cinnamon's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, which can alleviate symptoms of pertussis such as coughing and wheezing.

Antioxidant properties: Cinnamon's antioxidant properties can help protect against oxidative stress and damage caused by pertussis infection, potentially reducing the severity and duration of illness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective against respiratory tract inflammation associated with pertussis.
Barks: Containing flavonoids and tannins, which exhibit antimicrobial properties, helping to combat the bacterial cause of pertussis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cinnamomum verum bark at dawn when the dew is still present for optimal oil content.
Dry the harvested bark in a well-ventilated area under shade for 7-10 days or until it reaches 12% moisture content.
Steam distill the dried bark in a 1:5 ratio with distilled water for 4-6 hours to extract the essential oils.
Filter the extracted oil through a coffee filter or cotton cloth to remove impurities and sediment.
Store the final cinnamomum verum essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum essential oils helps with pertussis because of its potent antimicrobial properties. The oil's active compound, eucalyptol, has been shown to inhibit the growth of Bordetella pertussis, the bacteria that causes whooping cough. Additionally, the oil's decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties can help alleviate symptoms such as coughing fits, congestion, and chest tightness. By targeting multiple aspects of pertussis, Tasmanian blue gum essential oils may offer a natural and effective way to manage this respiratory infection.
remedy_properties: Decongestant: Tasmanian Blue Gum essential oil's decongestant properties help to reduce mucus production and congestion in the lungs, making it easier to breathe and alleviating symptoms of Pertussis.

Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of Tasmanian Blue Gum essential oil can help combat the bacterial infection caused by Bordetella pertussis, reducing the severity and duration of Pertussis symptoms.

Expectorant: The expectorant properties of Tasmanian Blue Gum essential oil help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to expel and relieving coughing fits associated with Pertussis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Tasmanian Blue Gum contain a high concentration of eucalyptol, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating respiratory infections such as pertussis.

Barks: The bark of the Tasmanian Blue Gum contains compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids that have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to combat the bacterial infection caused by pertussis.

Seeds: The seeds of the Tasmanian Blue Gum contain a significant amount of terpenes, which are known for their antispasmodic and expectorant properties, making it effective in relieving coughing fits associated with pertussis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves from mature eucalyptus globulus trees in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm wellventilated area for at least 48 hours or use a food dehydrator on low heat setting.
Crush the dried leaves into small particles using mortar and pestle or a spice grinder to release essential oils.
Steam distill the crushed leaves with distilled water in a steam distillation apparatus for 2-3 hours to separate essential oil from plant material.
Collect and filter the resulting essential oil through cheesecloth or coffee filters into dark glass containers for storage at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with pertussis because of its calming and soothing properties, which can help to reduce stress and anxiety in individuals experiencing symptoms of whooping cough. The oil's antimicrobial properties may also help to combat the Bordetella pertussis bacteria that causes the infection. Additionally, lavender's sedative effects can aid in promoting restful sleep and reducing the risk of further complications, such as bronchitis and pneumonia, making it a valuable natural remedy for those affected by pertussis.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: English Lavender essential oil has been shown to relax bronchial smooth muscle and reduce spasms, which can alleviate the severe coughing fits associated with Pertussis.

Anti-inflammatory property: English Lavender essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, making it easier for patients to breathe and recover from Pertussis.

Antimicrobial property: English Lavender essential oil exhibits antimicrobial activity against certain bacteria, including Streptococcus pneumoniae, which can contribute to secondary infections that often complicate Pertussis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lavender roots are rich in sesquiterpenes, which provide anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate respiratory issues like pertussis.

Flowers: The fragrant and aromatic flowers of English lavender contain linalool and linalyl acetate, which have been shown to exhibit calming effects on the respiratory system, making them effective for treating pertussis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavender flowers at peak blooming stage and dry them in shade or using a food dehydrator.
Chop dried lavender flowers into small pieces and place them in a clean glass jar with a lid.
Add 10% of the total volume of chopped lavender with carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily to allow infusion.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and retaining the infused oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice essential oils helps with pertussis because of its antimicrobial properties, which enable it to combat the Bordetella pertussis bacteria that causes the infection. The oil's natural anti-inflammatory compounds also soothe the respiratory tract and reduce inflammation, making it easier for the body to expel the bacteria. Additionally, licorice essential oils can help alleviate symptoms such as coughing and congestion, providing relief to those affected by pertussis.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Herbal licorice essential oil helps to loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory tract, relieving congestion and coughing associated with pertussis.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant compounds in herbal licorice essential oil help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the lungs, which can exacerbate pertussis symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal licorice essential oil's anti-inflammatory compounds have been shown to reduce swelling and irritation in the respiratory tract, helping to alleviate coughing, wheezing, and chest discomfort associated with pertussis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain a high concentration of glycyrrhizin, a compound that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of pertussis.

Barks: The barks of the licorice plant are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been found to exhibit antimicrobial and antiviral properties, making them effective against pertussis-causing bacteria.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered root with 200 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil at a ratio of 1:3.
Steep the mixture for 12 hours in a dark glass bottle, shaking occasionally, to allow the herbal properties to infuse into the oil.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to separate the infused oil from the solids and discard the solids.
Store the resulting essential oil in a cool, dark place for up to 6 months or use immediately after filtering.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ironwort essential oils helps with pertussis because of its antimicrobial properties, which target the bacteria that causes whooping cough. The oil's bioactive compounds, such as sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, exhibit potent antibacterial activity against Bordetella pertussis, effectively inhibiting its growth and reducing inflammation in the respiratory tract. Additionally, ironwort essential oils may also help to soothe symptoms like coughing and congestion by reducing oxidative stress and promoting immune response modulation.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Ironwort extracts have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity against a range of bacteria, including Bordetella pertussis, the causative agent of pertussis, which can help combat the infection.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The plant's extracts have anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce the severity of symptoms such as coughing and fever associated with pertussis.
Expectorant properties: Ironwort has been traditionally used to help relieve respiratory congestion and expel mucus, which can aid in reducing coughing fits and other symptoms of pertussis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, ironwort roots are valued for their ability to combat bacterial infections like pertussis.
Barks: Containing a unique blend of terpenoids and phenolic acids, ironwort barks help to boost the immune system and fight off respiratory pathogens.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of dried sideritis scardica herb from a trusted source.
Grind the dried herb into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine 50 milliliters of carrier oil such as olive or coconut oil with 5 grams of the powdered herb.
Place the mixture in a dark glass jar and steep it for 21 days at room temperature, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discard solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena essential oils helps with pertussis because of its natural antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's active compounds, such as citral and geraniol, have been shown to effectively combat the bacteria that cause pertussis (Bordetella pertussis) by inhibiting their growth and reducing inflammation in the respiratory tract. This helps alleviate symptoms such as coughing, sneezing, and congestion, providing relief to individuals infected with the disease.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Lemon verbena's antimicrobial compounds can help combat bacterial infections like pertussis (whooping cough), reducing the severity and duration of symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon verbena's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, which is common during pertussis infections, making breathing easier and relieving symptoms.

Expectorant properties: Lemon verbena's expectorant compounds can help loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to cough up and reducing congestion associated with pertussis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, making them effective against respiratory issues such as pertussis.
Seeds: Contain a high concentration of limonene, a compound that helps alleviate symptoms like coughing and wheezing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh verbena officinalis leaves and stems at dawn or dusk to preserve their potency.
Dry the harvested material using a warm air circulation method for 3-5 days.
Steam distill the dried herb using 10-15 grams of plant material and 100 ml of water for 2 hours.
Separate the essential oil from the distillation residue by decanting and filtering through coffee filters.
Store the obtained verbena officinalis essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hyssop essential oils helps with pertussis because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which help combat the bacteria responsible for this respiratory infection. The oil's expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties also ease congestion, coughing, and difficulty breathing commonly associated with pertussis. Additionally, hyssop's antibacterial activity targets the Bordetella pertussis bacteria, reducing the severity of symptoms and supporting the body's natural healing process. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to alleviate respiratory issues and can be a valuable adjunctive treatment in managing pertussis.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial property: Hyssop essential oil has been shown to exhibit antibacterial activity against a range of bacteria, including those responsible for pertussis, making it effective in combating infections and reducing the severity of symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory property: Hyssop oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, which is a common complication of pertussis, and alleviate coughing, congestion, and other respiratory issues associated with the disease.

Expectorant property: As an expectorant, hyssop essential oil can help loosen and clear out mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe and reducing the severity of symptoms such as coughing and wheezing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Hyssop roots contain valuable compounds like saponins and terpenoids, which are known for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective against pertussis-causing bacteria.

Seeds: Hyssop seeds have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues like whooping cough, and their essential oil is said to exhibit expectorant and mucolytic properties, helping to loosen and remove mucus from the airways.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hyssopus officinalis leaves in the morning after dewfall for optimal oil quality.
Steam-distill the harvested leaves using a copper still and a mixture of water and hyssop leaves at 100-110 degrees celsius for 3 hours.
Monitor distillation temperature and pressure to prevent contamination and ensure optimal oil yield.
Separate the essential oil from the hydrosol using a glass separator funnel and store it in a dark glass vial for up to 6 months.
Perform quality control tests on the essential oil by analyzing its chemical composition and sensory evaluation before labeling and packaging.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat pertussis with herbal essential oils is to diffuse a blend of eucalyptus, peppermint, and lavender oils in the patient's environment. This helps relieve respiratory symptoms such as coughing and congestion. Topical application of tea tree oil can also be used to soothe the skin and reduce inflammation. Additionally, inhaling the vapors of ginger and chamomile essential oils can help ease nausea and promote relaxation.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to pertussis that are treated with herbal essential oils are bronchitis, asthma, and chronic coughs. Essential oils like eucalyptus, peppermint, and thyme have natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties that help relieve congestion and ease respiratory discomfort. They can also be used to calm coughing spasms and reduce inflammation in the airways, providing a natural alternative or complement to conventional treatments for these conditions.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus essential oils helps with pertussis because of its decongestant and expectorant properties. It aids in relieving respiratory congestion and coughs, two primary symptoms associated with pertussis, commonly known as whooping cough. The oil's anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties also help to combat secondary infections that often accompany the disease. By alleviating these symptoms, Eucalyptus globulus essential oils can provide relief and promote recovery in individuals suffering from pertussis. Its application can be particularly beneficial during the acute phase of the illness.
remedy_constituents: Eucalyptol (1,8-cineole): It helps to relieve respiratory symptoms associated with pertussis, such as cough and congestion, by acting as an expectorant and thinning mucus.
α-Phellandrene: This monoterpene has antimicrobial properties that help combat the Bordetella pertussis bacteria that causes the disease, reducing the severity and duration of symptoms.
α-Pinene: As a bronchodilator, α-pinene helps to relax airway muscles, making it easier to breathe and reducing the severity of coughs associated with pertussis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the primary source of essential oil, which is used for its decongestant and expectorant properties to help alleviate respiratory issues like pertussis.
Barks: The bark is another significant source of essential oil, containing compounds like eucalyptol, which is used to help relieve respiratory symptoms associated with pertussis.
Stems: The stems of Eucalyptus globulus also yield essential oil, which is used for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties to help manage respiratory infections like pertussis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves from mature eucalyptus globulus trees ideally during cooler morning hours for optimal oil quality.
Steam distill the leaves in a ratio of 1 part leaves to 3 parts distilled water for 2-3 hours.
Collect the condensed steam that contains the essential oil in a separate container for 1-2 hours.
Separate the essential oil from the water through decantation or centrifugation to obtain a clear oil layer in 30 minutes.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter or a coffee filter to remove any remaining impurities in 10-15 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea essential oils helps with pertussis because it contains active compounds that stimulate the immune system and have anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's bioactive constituents, such as alkylamides and caffeic acid derivatives, have been shown to enhance the body's natural defense mechanisms against bacterial infections, including whooping cough. By modulating the immune response and reducing inflammation, Echinacea purpurea essential oils may help alleviate symptoms and support recovery from pertussis.
remedy_constituents: Cichoric acid: It has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce the severity of pertussis symptoms and alleviate coughing.
Isobutyl amide: This compound exhibits immunomodulatory activity, enhancing the body's immune response to fight off the Bordetella pertussis bacteria that cause pertussis.

Caffeic acid: It has antimicrobial properties, which help to inhibit the growth and proliferation of the Bordetella pertussis bacteria, thereby reducing the risk of infection and severity of symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used for their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe respiratory issues.
Flowers: They are used for their immune-boosting and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate respiratory problems such as pertussis.
Leaves: They are used for their antimicrobial and antioxidant properties, which can help fight off infections and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of fresh echinacea purpurea flowers when the petals have just begun to fade from their vibrant color.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm well-ventilated area for 7 to 10 days to reduce their moisture content.
Steam distill the dried flowers in a steam distillation apparatus at a temperature of 100 to 110 degrees celsius for 2 to 3 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil and filter it through a coffee filter to remove any impurities or sediment.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle sealed with a tight-fitting lid to preserve its potency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea angustifolia essential oils helps with pertussis because it possesses antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that aid in combating the underlying bacterial infection responsible for the condition. The immunomodulatory effects of Echinacea angustifolia stimulate the body's natural defense mechanisms, enhancing its ability to fight off the infection. Additionally, its expectorant properties help relieve congestion and coughing associated with pertussis, making it a potential complementary therapy for managing symptoms and promoting recovery. Its antiviral properties may also help reduce the severity of the condition.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds help boost the immune system, reducing the severity and duration of pertussis by enhancing the body's natural defenses.
Alkylamides: Alkylamides exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate the respiratory symptoms associated with pertussis, such as coughing and congestion.

Alkaloids (specifically, Cichoric acid is not an alkaloid, but rather a sesquiterpene lactone): Some alkaloids present in Echinacea angustifolia have antimicrobial properties, which can help combat the Bordetella pertussis bacteria responsible for pertussis.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are a primary source of essential oils due to their rich content of bioactive compounds, such as alkylamides and caffeic acid derivatives.
Leaves: Leaves are also commonly used for essential oil production, as they contain similar bioactive compounds found in the roots.
Stems: Stems are sometimes used for essential oil production, although the yield and quality may vary compared to roots and leaves.
remedy_recipe: Harvest echinacea angustifolia roots in the fall when the plant is dormant at a rate of 10 pounds per gallon.
Clean the roots thoroughly to remove dirt and debris in cold running water for about 10 minutes.
Steam distill the cleaned roots in a still at 100 degrees celsius for 2 hours to extract oil.
Separate the essential oil from water using a condenser and collect it in a container.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter to remove any impurities and achieve clarity.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris essential oils helps with pertussis because of its antimicrobial properties, particularly its ability to target and destroy the Bordetella pertussis bacteria responsible for the disease. Thyme oil's high concentration of thymol, a phenolic compound, makes it effective against a wide range of pathogens, including bacteria, viruses, and fungi. By inhibiting the growth of these microorganisms, Thymus vulgaris essential oils may help alleviate symptoms, reduce the duration, and prevent the spread of pertussis.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Thymol has antimicrobial properties that help combat the Bordetella pertussis bacteria responsible for pertussis, reducing the severity and duration of the illness.
Carvacrol: Carvacrol has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects that help alleviate respiratory symptoms associated with pertussis, such as coughing and congestion.
P-cymene: P-cymene has antimicrobial and expectorant properties that help loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, reducing the severity of pertussis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: These are the most commonly used part of Thyme for essential oil production due to their high concentration of thymol, a compound with antimicrobial properties.
Flowers: Thyme flowers are used to produce essential oils that help alleviate respiratory issues, including pertussis, as they contain compounds that have antiseptic and expectorant properties.

Stems: The stems of Thyme plants are used for essential oil production, particularly for their high content of borneol and borneol acetate, which exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest a sufficient quantity of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers at the peak of their fragrance.
Steam distill the harvested thymus vulgaris plant material using a distillation apparatus at 100°c for 2 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil from the distillation apparatus and filter it using a paper filter.
Store the filtered thymus vulgaris essential oil in a dark glass bottle sealed with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature.
Label and date the thymus vulgaris essential oil bottle and store it in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils helps with pertussis because of its potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil contains compounds that have been shown to inhibit the growth of Bordetella pertussis, the bacteria responsible for whooping cough. Additionally, the oil's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus, relieving congestion and coughing. Its immunomodulatory effects also support the body's natural defense against the infection, promoting a quicker recovery and reducing the severity of symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: Cineole has antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial infections associated with pertussis, such as Bordetella pertussis.
Limonene: Limonene exhibits expectorant properties, which can help relieve respiratory congestion and coughing associated with pertussis.
Pinene: Pinene has bronchodilatory effects, helping to relax and open airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing the severity of coughing fits in pertussis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are primarily used due to their high concentration of oils, particularly cineole.
Buds: They are used for their high oil content, which is rich in cineole, a key component of Tea Tree Oil.
Stems: They are used as a secondary source of oil, although the yield is generally lower than that of leaves and buds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of melaleuca alternifolia at dawn when the plant is at its peak oil content.
Steam distill the harvested leaves at 100°c for 2-3 hours to release the oil from the plant material.
Collect the distilled oil and separate it from the water using a condenser and a separator.
Filter the essential oil through a 0.2 micron filter to remove any impurities or residue.
Store the final essential oil in a dark glass bottle at room temperature to preserve its potency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils helps with pertussis because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. It contains compounds like gingerol and shogaol that have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate respiratory issues associated with the disease. Additionally, the oil's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing the severity of coughing fits. This makes Zingiber officinale essential oils a potential natural remedy to help manage pertussis symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Beta-sesquiphellandrene: It helps in reducing inflammation and congestion in the respiratory tract, which is beneficial in alleviating the symptoms of pertussis.
Zingiberene: It possesses antimicrobial properties, which help in fighting off the Bordetella pertussis bacteria responsible for the infection.
Caryophyllene oxide: It has anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which help in reducing the severity of the cough and other symptoms associated with pertussis.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of ginger are the most commonly used part for essential oil production, as they contain the highest concentration of active compounds, including gingerols and shogaols.
Roots: The roots of ginger are also used for essential oil production, particularly for their high content of gingerols and other bioactive compounds.
Stems: Some essential oil producers may use the stems of ginger for oil production, as they contain smaller amounts of bioactive compounds, but still have some therapeutic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes at the end of their growing season when the foliage starts to yellow.
Wash the harvested rhizomes with distilled water to remove any dirt or debris from the surface.
Steam distill the washed rhizomes in a glass still at 100-150°c for 2-3 hours to extract the oil.
Collect the distilled oil and separate it from the water using a separatory funnel or decanting.
Store the extracted oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid and a label indicating the date and contents.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea pallida essential oils helps with pertussis because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which aid in fighting off the Bordetella pertussis bacteria responsible for the illness. The oil's anti-inflammatory properties also help reduce congestion and coughing, providing relief to those suffering from pertussis. Additionally, its immunomodulatory effects may help boost the body's natural defense against the infection, promoting faster recovery and reducing the duration of the illness. This natural remedy offers a promising approach to alleviating pertussis symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Icariin: This flavonoid has anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate the respiratory inflammation associated with pertussis.
Alkylamides: These terpenoid compounds have immunomodulatory effects, enhancing the body's immune response to fight off the Bordetella pertussis bacteria.
Cichoric acid: This phenolic compound has antimicrobial properties, which can help inhibit the growth of the Bordetella pertussis bacteria and reduce the severity of the infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: Known for their anti-inflammatory properties, Echinacea pallida roots are commonly used to create essential oils that promote immune system support.
Flowers: Echinacea pallida flowers are used to make essential oils that have antiseptic and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of various respiratory issues.
Barks: Echinacea pallida barks are used to create essential oils that have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to combat bacterial and viral infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-40 grams of fresh echinacea pallida flowers in the early morning when dew is still present.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm place with good air circulation for 7-10 days.
Steam distill the dried flowers for 1-2 hours using a distillation apparatus with a 10:1 water to plant ratio.
Collect the distilled essential oil in a clean glass container and store it in a cool dark place.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter or cheesecloth to remove any impurities and sediment.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils helps with pertussis because of its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's ability to combat bacterial and viral infections can help alleviate symptoms of pertussis, such as coughing and congestion. Its calming effects can also help soothe respiratory discomfort and reduce the severity of coughing fits. Additionally, the oil's expectorant properties can aid in loosening and clearing mucus, promoting easier breathing and potentially shortening the duration of the illness. This natural remedy can be a valuable adjunct to conventional treatment.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, helping to ease the intense coughing fits associated with pertussis.
Linalyl acetate: This terpene has a sedative effect, promoting relaxation and reducing stress, which can help alleviate symptoms of pertussis such as anxiety and insomnia.
Limonene: This terpene has antimicrobial properties, helping to prevent the spread of the Bordetella pertussis bacteria and reduce the severity of the infection.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are the primary part used to produce essential oils due to their high concentration of lavender oil compounds.
Leaves: Some essential oil manufacturers use leaves in addition to flowers to increase oil yield and improve quality.
Stems: Stems may be used for distillation, although their contribution to the final essential oil is often less significant than that of flowers.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the flower spikes of lavandula angustifolia in the early morning after the dew has evaporated and before the heat intensifies.
Dry the fresh flower spikes in a warm well-ventilated area for 1-2 weeks to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill 100g of dried flower spikes with 1 liter of distilled water at 100°c for 2-3 hours to separate essential oil from water.
Collect the essential oil that separates from the distilled water and store it in a dark glass bottle for future use.
Filter the collected essential oil through a paper filter to remove any impurities and improve its clarity and stability.

remedies.remedy_desc: Pelargonium sidoides essential oils helps with pertussis because of its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil contains compounds that have been shown to have immunomodulatory effects, which can help to boost the body's natural defense against the Bordetella pertussis bacteria that causes the disease. Additionally, the oil's expectorant properties can help to relieve congestion and coughing, making it easier to manage the symptoms of pertussis. This natural remedy has been used in traditional medicine to support respiratory health.
remedy_constituents: Ascorbic acid: Helps reduce the severity and duration of pertussis by its antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate oxidative stress in the body.
Ursolic acid: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate coughing and other respiratory symptoms associated with pertussis.
Rosmarinic acid: Demonstrates antimicrobial properties, which may help combat infections caused by pertussis-causing bacteria.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary part used due to their high concentration of medicinal compounds, such as phenolic acids and flavonoids, which have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: They are also used to make essential oils, containing compounds that may help alleviate symptoms of pertussis, such as coughs and respiratory issues.
Stems: The stems of Pelargonium sidoides contain compounds with potential antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which may contribute to their use in essential oils for pertussis treatment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 kg of fresh pelargonium sidoides leaves and flowers in the early morning when they are at their peak potency.
Steam distill the harvested plant material at 100°c for 3 hours to release the essential oil.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the water using a separatory funnel.
Filter the collected essential oil through a 0.2 micron filter paper to remove any impurities.
Bottle the filtered essential oil in a dark glass container and store it in a cool dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils helps with pertussis because of its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in reducing the severity and duration of the disease. The oil's expectorant properties help relieve coughing and congestion, making it easier for patients to breathe and recover. Additionally, its antioxidant properties may help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by the infection, promoting overall well-being and faster recovery. This makes Rosmarinus officinalis essential oil a potentially beneficial complementary therapy for pertussis treatment.
remedy_constituents: Caryophyllene oxide: It has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce inflammation and prevent secondary bacterial infections associated with pertussis.

Rosmarinic acid: It has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe and alleviate symptoms of pertussis.

Bornyl acetate: It has expectorant and bronchodilatory properties, which can help loosen and clear mucus from the airways and improve breathing, making it easier to manage the cough and respiratory symptoms of pertussis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are commonly used to make essential oils because they contain a high concentration of fragrant oils.
Flowers: Flowers are used to make essential oils because they have a more delicate and intense aroma than the leaves.
Stems: Stems are sometimes used to make essential oils, particularly when the leaves and flowers are not available.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves at the peak of their potency when they are in full bloom.
Steam distill the harvested leaves at a temperature of 100-110°c for 2-3 hours to release the oil.
Separate the rosmarinus officinalis essential oil from the distilled water through decantation or centrifugation.
Filter the essential oil through a 10-micron filter to remove impurities and debris.
Store the rosmarinus officinalis essential oil in a dark glass bottle away from direct sunlight.
